auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management. Use `auth login` to configure API key, `auth status` to check, `auth test` to verify, `auth refresh` for token refresh.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.
login.usage_instructions: Configure auth credentials. Use --profile/-p for specific profile. Use --force/-F to re-authenticate.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Clear stored credentials. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management for multiple stores or environments.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Create a new profile with NAME (required positional).

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Delete a profile by NAME. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Get profile details by NAME (required positional).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.
list.usage_instructions: List all profiles. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

status.usage_instructions: Check auth status. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Test that credentials work. Use --verbose/-v for details.

cache.usage_instructions: Manage response cache. Use `cache clear` to remove cached responses.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.

catalog.usage_instructions: Browse the Brick Owl catalog -- lookup items, search, get IDs, check availability, view inventory, colors, conditions, categories, and themes.

availability.help: Get pricing and availability for a catalog item.
availability.usage_instructions: Check item availability. BOID is required positional.

colors.usage_instructions: List available colors for an item. BOID is required positional.

id.help: Look up a catalog item by external ID. ID types: item_no, design_id, bl_item_no, set_number (alias: bricklink -> bl_item_no)
id.usage_instructions: Get Brick Owl ID for an item. ITEM_ID is required positional.

options.name: --id-type
options.help: ID type: item_no, design_id, bl_item_no, set_number (alias: bricklink -> bl_item_no)

inventory.help: Get the parts inventory for a set.
inventory.usage_instructions: View inventory for a catalog item. BOID is required positional.

arguments.name: boid
arguments.help: Brick Owl item ID (BOID) for a set

lookup.help: Look up a catalog item by BOID.
lookup.usage_instructions: Look up catalog item by BOID (required positional). Returns item details.

search.usage_instructions: Search catalog. QUERY is required positional.

coupon.usage_instructions: Manage coupons -- list, get, create user/code coupons, delete.

create-code.help: Create a coupon with a specific code.
create-code.usage_instructions: Create a code-based coupon. Requires code and discount details.

create-user.help: Create a coupon for a specific user.
create-user.usage_instructions: Create a user-specific coupon. Requires user details.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a coupon. COUPON_ID is required positional.

get.help: Get details for a specific coupon.
get.usage_instructions: Get coupon details. COUPON_ID is required positional.

list.usage_instructions: List coupons. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of coupons to return

inventory.usage_instructions: Store inventory management -- list, get, search, stats, update, and delete inventory items.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete an inventory item. LOT_ID is required positional.

get.help: Get details for a specific inventory lot.
get.usage_instructions: Get inventory item details. LOT_ID is required positional.

list.usage_instructions: List store inventory. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --type
options.help: Filter by item type (Part, Set, Minifigure, etc.)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of lots to return

search.help: Search inventory for a part by number. Looks up the part via catalog ID, then filters your inventory lots matching that item. Returns bricklink-compatible output format.
search.usage_instructions: Search inventory. QUERY is required positional.

arguments.name: part_no
arguments.help: Part number to search for (design ID, BL item no, etc.)

options.name: --color
options.help: Filter by Brick Owl color ID

stats.help: Show inventory statistics. Computes totals for lots, items, and value across all active inventory.

update.usage_instructions: Update an inventory item. LOT_ID is required positional.

issue.usage_instructions: Run `brickowl issue --help` for exact arguments and options.

get.help: Get one issue report by order ID.
get.usage_instructions: Run `brickowl issue get --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: order_id
arguments.help: The order ID of the issue report

list.help: List issue reports (browser-based). Shows open issue reports (disputes) filed by customers.
list.usage_instructions: Run `brickowl issue list --help` for exact arguments and options.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of reports to return

resolve.help: Resolve an issue report for a specific order (browser-based). Finds the issue report matching the given order ID and clicks the resolve action.
resolve.usage_instructions: Run `brickowl issue resolve --help` for exact arguments and options.

arguments.name: order_id
arguments.help: The order ID of the issue report to resolve

messages.usage_instructions: Manage Brick Owl messages -- list, get, send, reply, mark read/unread.

get.usage_instructions: Get a message. MESSAGE_ID is required positional.

list.usage_instructions: List messages. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --include-history
options.help: Include message history thread for each message

mark-read.usage_instructions: Mark a message as read. MESSAGE_ID is required.

mark-unread.usage_instructions: Mark a message as unread. MESSAGE_ID is required.

examples: brickowl messages reply 12345678 "Thank you for your message!"
brickowl messages reply 12345678 "Here is the tracking info" --copy
reply.usage_instructions: Reply to a message. MESSAGE_ID is required.

options.name: --priority
options.help: Send as high priority (accepted for parity)

options.name: --copy
options.help: Send a copy to yourself (accepted for parity)

send.help: Send a message linked to an order (browser-based). Looks up the buyer username from the order, then sends a message.
examples: brickowl messages send 12345678 "Your order has been shipped!"
send.usage_instructions: Send a new message. Requires recipient and content.

arguments.name: order_id
arguments.help: Order ID to send message for

options.name: --priority
options.help: Send as high priority (accepted for parity)

options.name: --copy
options.help: Send a copy to yourself (accepted for parity)

order.usage_instructions: Order management -- list, get, view items, check status, ship, set tracking, and add notes to orders.

get.help: Get details for a specific order.
get.usage_instructions: Get order details by ORDER_ID (required positional).

items.usage_instructions: List items in an order. ORDER_ID is required positional.

list.help: List orders from the Brick Owl store.
list.usage_instructions: List orders. Use --status/-s to filter. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --status
options.help: Filter by status (name, ID, or comma-separated)

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of orders to return

note.help: Set seller note on an order.
examples: brickowl order note 12345678 "Shipped via USPS Priority"
note.usage_instructions: Add a note to an order. ORDER_ID and NOTE are required.

ship.help: Mark an order as shipped, optionally with tracking.
ship.usage_instructions: Mark an order as shipped. ORDER_ID is required.

status.help: Set the status of an order. Status IDs: 0 = Pending, 1 = Payment Submitted, 2 = Payment Received, 3 = Processing, 4 = Processed, 5 = Shipped, 6 = Received, 7 = On Hold, 8 = Cancelled
status.usage_instructions: Check order status. ORDER_ID is required positional.

tracking.help: Add tracking information to an order.
tracking.usage_instructions: Set tracking number for an order. ORDER_ID and TRACKING are required.

quotes.usage_instructions: Manage quotes -- list, get, and submit quotes.

get.help: Get details for a specific quote.
get.usage_instructions: Get quote details. QUOTE_ID is required positional.

list.usage_instructions: List quotes. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of quotes to return

submit.help: Submit a shipping quote for a quote request.
examples: brickowl quotes submit QUOTE_ID 3.50 --note "Ships within 2 business days"
submit.usage_instructions: Submit a quote. QUOTE_ID is required positional.

arguments.name: quote_id
arguments.help: The quote ID to submit shipping amount for

refund.usage_instructions: Manage refunds -- get refund info, issue partial refunds, or full refunds.

full.help: Issue a full refund for an order.
full.usage_instructions: Issue a full refund. ORDER_ID is required positional.

info.help: Get refund information for an order. Shows order total, prior refunds, and maximum refund amount.
info.usage_instructions: Get refund info for an order. ORDER_ID is required positional.

issue.help: Issue a partial refund for an order.
examples: brickowl refund issue 12345678 5.00 --reason "Overcharged Shipping" --message "Refunding excess shipping"
issue.usage_instructions: Issue a partial refund. ORDER_ID and AMOUNT are required.

details.help: Get your Brick Owl user/store details.
details.usage_instructions: Get user details. USER_ID is optional (defaults to self).

usage_instructions: Use for all Brick Owl operations -- orders, inventory, catalog, messages, refunds, coupons, quotes, and user info. Commands follow `brickowl <group> <action>` pattern. Most list commands support --table/-t, --filter/-f, --limit/-l, and --properties/-p.
